<summary id=22lprf30>
DM diagnostic third mode (tilt), figure placement, and Figure 1 promotion — Faber2026 co-detection manuscript (2026-07-15)

## Narrative record

**Discriminate-figure cleanup and final render (opening).** Continuing from the discriminate-mode dynamic-spectrum work, the agent added a common-x-limit fix to the discriminate panels (`axes[row, col].set_xlim(-window_ms / 2, window_ms / 2)`) in `/Users/jakobfaber/Developer/repos/github.com/jakobtfaber/Faber2026/pipeline/dispersion/dm_campaign/render_dm_zoom_comparison.py`. Read: CHIME @ DSA-DM tilts −1.09 ms while CHIME @ CHIME-DM is upright (CHIME resolves the two DMs); both DSA panels identical (tilt +0.03 ms ≈ 1 sample, DSA cannot). Then generated two final figures into `analysis/dm-joint-phase-v2/results/diagnostics/`: `dm_zoom_discriminate.png` (phineas+isha, window-ms 10, 5,287,544 bytes) and `dm_zoom_adopted_all.png` (all 12, window-ms 8, 20,718,051 bytes), copied to workspace.

**Submodule placement discovery.** Confirmed script durably in submodule working tree (untracked `??`), but the two figures landed in the superproject `analysis/` tree, not the submodule. Explored submodule for a figure home: found `analysis/scattering-dm-locked-2026-07-14`, `analysis/chime_dm`, and `dispersion/dm_campaign/` files. Key finding: submodule `.gitignore` line 139 is `*.png` — the pipeline repo deliberately does NOT track PNGs; the generator script is the tracked deliverable. Placed figure copies in a new `dispersion/dm_campaign/diagnostics/` dir (both PNGs), confirmed via `git check-ignore` that the PNG is gitignored but the script is not.

**USER (e11):** "The DSA "cross" plots (3rd over from the left) look like the correct DM's to me"

The agent flagged this as the trap the figure warns against. Quantified discriminating power: for phineas dDM=+0.0565, CHIME tilt +1.099 ms (429 samples) vs DSA tilt +32.1 µs (1.0 sample); for isha dDM=−0.1170, CHIME tilt −2.275 ms (889 samples) vs DSA −66.4 µs (2.0 samples). Explanation: columns 1-vs-2 (CHIME at each DM) are the DISCRIMINATING test — only CHIME resolves the DM and prefers CHIME-DM; columns 3-vs-4 (DSA at each DM) are a CONSISTENCY test only — both vertical/identical, DSA passing confirms CHIME-DM doesn't misalign DSA but cannot select a DM. Also explained CHIME bursts look diffuse due to scattering (τ∝ν⁻⁴), not DM error; and a legitimate caveat: for drifting sub-structure bursts, the structure-maximizing DM (phase-coherence) can differ slightly from cold-plasma DM. Proposed a sub-band peak-time (tilt) plot as the honest quantitative version.

**USER (e19):** "Are the DSA "cross" plots not the DSA bursts dedispersed to the CHIME-optimal DM?"

Confirmed: column 3 = DSA dedispersed to CHIME-optimal DM, column 4 = DSA at its own DSA-optimal DM. Reiterated column 3 vertical is a consistency check (passes) but not discriminating because column 4 is identical (DM difference moves DSA burst 1–2 samples, sub-visible).

**USER (e22):** "I think we should trust our CHIME-derived DM's more than we trust our DSA-derived DM's"

Agent confirmed this is exactly the current manuscript policy. Three-leg evidence stack: (1) precision — CHIME σ narrower in all 12, σ_DSA/σ_CHIME 1.4×–17×, median 7.4×; (2) discriminating power — 429-vs-1 sample tilt asymmetry, 34× leverage; (3) unbiasedness — injection-recovery CHIME bias +1×10⁻⁴ RMSE 3×10⁻⁴, ~25× tighter than DSA. Noted adopted σ = CHIME band σ (not widened joint σ); the structure-maximizing vs cold-plasma DM caveat is orthogonal. Nothing changes in `dm_measurements_table.tex` or catalog. Recorded endorsement to memory as `mem_efc2ba79d72a` (flagged similar to `mem_9333f0ec2c38`; left all rows in place).

**USER (e30):** "Add the third mode to the same script. I'll reiterate that we should make sure these artifacts get pushed to `Faber2026` or `Faber2026/pipeline/`"

**Tilt mode implementation.** Added to `render_dm_zoom_comparison.py`: helper `_delay_ms(delta_dm, f_mhz, f_ref_mhz)` (cold-plasma residual delay), `_subband_arrival_times(band, target_dm, window_s, n_sub)` (measures per-sub-band on-pulse arrival time after dedispersing to target_dm; band-integrated reference peak with light boxcar smooth `smooth = max(1, int(round(1.0e-4 / dt_s)))`; sub-bands below peak S/N 3.0 → NaN; 3-point parabolic peak refinement; returns sub_freq, arrival, ref_freq, dt_s), and `render_tilt(fits_path, out_path, bursts, window_ms, n_sub=6)`. Wired `--mode {adopted,discriminate,tilt}` and `--n-sub` (default 6) into CLI. Colors CH_COLOR (CHIME `#1f5fbf`), DS_COLOR (DSA `#e08214`).

First probe (phineas, isha, window-ms 12, n-sub 6): theory lines correct (CHIME two-DM curves fan out ~1–2 ms; DSA coincident 0.03–0.07 ms) but measured data points noise-limited (CHIME phineas only 2 blue points, low-freq below S/N; DSA scatter exceeds sub-sample split). Punchline recorded: per-sub-band arrival-time fitting is S/N- and scattering-limited, which is why the campaign used phase-coherence.

**Estimator improvements.** Added central-window peak search (`guard = int(0.20 * ntime)`, search central 60%) to kill scattered low-freq edge peaks; added a residual-scatter annotation (later removed). Re-rendered probe2 (phineas, isha, hamilton, n-sub 4): discovered per-panel autoscaling was misleading — DSA zoomed to ±0.01 ms vs CHIME ±1 ms, manufacturing fake "resolvable" structure. Honest fix: shared x-scale per burst row set by CHIME-band split (`chime_split = abs(_sweep_ms(dm_of["chime"] - dm_of["dsa"], 400.0, 800.0))`, `xlim = max(0.25, 1.4 * chime_split)`); replaced misleading verdict with `"DM split in band\n{abs(sweep):.3f} ms"` annotation. Removed unused `_own_resid`. Probe3 rendered correctly: CHIME panels fan two DM curves apart, DSA collapses both onto vertical stripe at zero, hamilton (ΔDM≈0) vertical everywhere (control).

**Figure tracking convention resolved.** Superproject `figures/` DOES track PDF/PNG/SVG (unlike the submodule which gitignores PNGs). So: script → pipeline submodule, figures → superproject `figures/`. Generated final `figures/dm_subband_tilt.png` (tilt, phineas/isha/hamilton, window-ms 12, n-sub 4, 320,891 bytes) and refreshed `figures/dm_zoom_discriminate.png`. Saved `dm_subband_tilt.png` as artifact: artifact_id `bf50294d-2232-4d0c-a545-97b40fd3caec`, version_id `dd371f5a-a39b-404d-aadf-438600226a32`.

**Push preparation and two PRs.** Confirmed remotes: Faber2026 origin `https://github.com/jakobtfaber/Faber2026.git` on branch `ms/census-remediation` @ `baac14ab22d9b3a57644ca56f657ac23f2edcc28`, default `main`, public; FLITS submodule origin `https://github.com/jakobtfaber/dsa110-FLITS.git`, HEAD `4e951c8acd6f0e221058d86ed97bb52b9d8c8597`, default `main`. GITHUB_TOKEN present (len 40). Asked user via ask_user how to scope the push; user chose **"Script→FLITS main, tilt figure→Faber2026 main (recommended)"** (skip the 5.3 MB discriminate PNG). Base64-encoded payloads (script 27,029 b64 bytes; tilt 427,857).

Opened two PRs via GitHub REST API (blob→tree→commit→ref→PR):
- **FLITS PR #185** (`https://github.com/jakobtfaber/dsa110-FLITS/pull/185`): script → `dispersion/dm_campaign/`, branch `agent/dm-zoom-diagnostic-script`, base main, blob `0c0bf7126d685fa60800a60ef152c65d1bebb1b6`, tree `4acc00578212536b5eb989963aec107465c9138f`, commit `60b50adb81e9a6e117ee2f95dd3054d9836e8eda`.
- **Faber2026 PR #83** (`https://github.com/jakobtfaber/Faber2026/pull/83`): `figures/dm_subband_tilt.png`, branch `agent/dm-subband-tilt-figure`, base main tip `8a4457dbf2a6d3105622293e42547b95bc6f92c9`, blob `ea17d0e3ae8ba462a1dd8f8e4829d5a97cc72fb8`, tree `d9b3e8579adb5245a8dc94471000942aa1366117`, commit `2afcaa0c969bf691164b74c95202b32e49eb264a` (this PR-creation bash cell was backgrounded exec_id `3b4299cb-b9dd-451f-bc35-d988f93fa275`, collected via wait_for_notification).

**USER (e76):** "Now that we have a solid definition for the DM across events and good provenance, we need to generate Figure 1 with the correct DM's, and remove the placeholder in the manuscript currently."

**Figure 1 investigation.** Figure 1 = `fig:codetection-data-grid` in `sections/observations.tex:37`, currently an `[AUTHOR REVIEW PENDING --- FIGURE 1]` fbox placeholder. Preceding prose (lines 31–35) says the gallery and joint data/model/residual panels are "withheld... while they pass the author figure-review gate." Discovered the full governance machinery:
- Generator `scripts/plot_codetection_data_grid.py` (reads `analysis/dm-joint-phase-v2/manuscript_dm_catalog.csv`, `load_adopted_dms` asserts adopted==CHIME to atol 5e-7, `DISPLAY_FACTORS = {"dsa": (12, 1), "chime": (1, 13)}`, residual_dm = target_dm − product_dm; imports from `plot_codetection_gallery`, `plot_codetection_triptych`, `flits.batch`).
- Fail-closed two-PR review workflow (`figure_review/README.md`, `figure_review/slots.json`, `scripts/figure_review.py`): new-batch → decide approved (owner name+note) → promote (writes SHA-256 receipt to `figure_review/approval_receipts/`). `figure_review.py verify` runs in `make test-science` (Makefile:30), fails if included TeX has `\includegraphics` of a protected target without a matching receipt. README: "automated checks, PR creation, and agent review never imply approval." As of this span: no batches, no receipts existed. Slot `fig1-gallery` target `figures/codetection_data_grid.pdf`. `verify` reads only receipts + included TeX + protected targets (slots.json), NOT the batch dir. `figures/codetection_data_grid.pdf` already tracked (committed in 96aaa85). Branch `ms/figure1-data-grid` exists. 24 npy products present.

**Rendering env setup.** Attempted regen with py312 → SciencePlots fell back to matplotlib defaults (not faithful; SciencePlots declared in `pipeline/pyproject.toml:17`). pip install into py312 blocked (read-only site-packages). Created managed conda env `fig1-render` (python 3.12: numpy, matplotlib<3.11, scienceplots, pyyaml, scipy, pandas). Style needs no LaTeX (`text.usetex=False`). `flits.__init__` pulls scattering→burstfit needing emcee; installed emcee, lmfit, h5py, tqdm, corner, astropy. Regenerated into isolated `/tmp/fig1_candidate/figures/` (PYTHONPATH=scripts:pipeline): PDF 7,433,536 bytes, PNG 9,033,815, SVG 10,854,906, matplotlib 3.10.9. PDF SHA-256 = `ba56453f63fee66580bfafd50f9b4829b39901b773d3de4bd464ced0ff697d8a`. Verified visually via downscaled preview (queued for vision, saved as artifact version_id `efb0566e-26a2-4ae1-8003-051bb91f047c`): all 12 bursts chronological, CHIME magenta / DSA black profiles, waterfall + spectrum marginal, inter-band gap wedge, RFI gray masks; phineas CHIME diffuse (scattering, expected); some panels show DSA peak time offset from CHIME (expected, flagged for owner review).

**Gate mechanics confirmed.** `figure_review.py verify` (`command_verify`): protected = slot targets; reads receipts from `RECEIPTS`; `approval_errors(included_tex(), protected, receipts)` raises SystemExit on error. `new-batch` args: batch_id, --title, --source-revision (default HEAD), --candidate-root (default ROOT), --read-from-revision, --pipeline-revision (required), --pipeline-repo (default ROOT/pipeline), --initial-status, --reviewer, --note. `command_new_batch` stages ALL governed figures at once (39 slot targets total: fig1-gallery, fig5-association, fig6-scint-summary, 12 dsa-acf-{nick}, 12 jointmodel_pair, 12 codetection_triptych, oran calibration), reads evidence from git revisions (7 evidence_specs: dm-catalog, joint-render-manifest [parent]; joint-fit-roster, joint-fit-adjudication, scint-component-catalog, scint-fit-catalog, oran-qualification [pipeline]). Agent stopped at the approval line — did not self-approve or remove placeholder; laid out the honest constraint that promotion is the owner's decision reserved by the gate. Recorded gate mechanics to memory (`mem_6212be425c54`, body truncated at 1000 chars).

At span end: agent had verified all 39 slot targets exist on disk, was about to ask the user how to clear the gate (the ask_user with "Approve now; I run the full formal workflow" option and the subsequent full workflow occur AFTER this span).
